summary: Improved data matching for the Inishie1 stage source by correcting string literals, assertion text, and stage data callback table entries in src/melee/gr/grinishie1.c. The PR replaced placeholder NULLs in grI1_803E4950 with actual grInishie1 function pointers, adjusted OSReport/__assert strings to match expected embedded data, removed reliance on a separate grI1_803E49A8 filename symbol by using the literal "grinishie1.c" directly, and corrected one assert expression string from "new_gobj" to "map_gobj".
what_changed: Changed the invalid_state macro's OSReport text from using filename "grinishie1.c" and message "%s:%d: oioi..." to using the condition-like string "i!=HATENA_APPEAR_CHECKLOOP" and message "%s:%d: oioi..\n".

Filled the grI1_803E4950 stage data function-pointer slots that were previously NULL with grI1_803E48F4, grInishie1_801FA90C, grInishie1_801FA908, grInishie1_801FA984, grInishie1_801FA988, grInishie1_801FA9AC, grInishie1_801FCBB0, and grInishie1_801FCBB8.
Commented out the standalone char array grI1_803E49A8[] = "grinishie1.c".
Changed an OSReport call in grInishie1_801FA9B4 to pass the literal "grinishie1.c" instead of the grI1_803E49A8 symbol.
Adjusted an assertion message in grInishie1_801FB3F0_inline from "%s:%d: oioi..." to "%s:%d: oioi..\n".
Corrected the assertion expression string in fn_801FBF6C from "new_gobj" to "map_gobj".
smart_moves: Matched data by treating diagnostic strings as part of the binary layout, including small punctuation and newline differences.
Restored the stage data table with actual function pointers instead of leaving callback slots as NULL, which likely improved rodata/data matching while preserving known symbols.
Avoided keeping an unnecessary filename string symbol when the callsite could use the literal directly, reducing stray data that would interfere with matching.
Corrected assert expression text to the source-level condition name expected by the original binary rather than a semantically similar but mismatching string.
decomp_lessons: For data-matching PRs, assertion and OSReport strings must be matched exactly; ellipsis count, newline characters, and condition strings can matter.
Stage data tables should be audited for placeholder NULL function-pointer entries once candidate lifecycle/callback functions are known.
A separate named string object is not always correct even when the bytes match; literal placement and symbolization can affect data ordering and matching.
Assert messages often encode the original expression text, not necessarily the decompiler author's current variable or helper name.
naming_conventions: Stage-specific symbols use the grInishie1_ prefix for functions and grI1_ prefix for data.
Unknown or address-based functions retain address-derived names such as grInishie1_801FA908 and fn_801FBF6C.
Stage data object naming follows the address-style convention grI1_803E4950.
assembly_or_matching_tactics: Replace placeholder NULL entries in data tables with the actual referenced function symbols to match expected pointer data.
Use exact string literals for OSReport and __assert, including newline escape sequences and punctuation.
Inline a filename literal at the callsite when a separate global char array creates mismatching data.
Check assert expression strings against the original binary data rather than inferring them from nearby variable names.
follow_up_queries: Search other grinishie1 assert and OSReport strings for exact punctuation/newline mismatches.
Audit other stage data structs for placeholder NULL callback slots that may need real function pointers.
Check whether commented-out string globals similar to grI1_803E49A8 remain in nearby ground stage files and affect data matching.
Compare assert expression strings in ground item-spawn helpers for source-condition names versus inferred variable names.
